subject: ci/build: restore CI_GITHUB_TOKEN release-please + update deps to latest, zero audit findings
decision: org PAT over GitHub App for release-please.
why: matches the jbcom-wide standard; the App path required per-repo

decision: Renderer signals+bindings architecture (koota-native), NOT embedded renderers or an imperative RenderBackend bundled with three
why: To make 2D/3D fundamental enough to 'plug into anything', the engine core must emit reactive SIGNALS (koota traits: PlacementState/WorldPosition/etc.) and renderer BINDINGS subscribe+reconcile — exactly the koota+react-bindings model. The core imports NO renderer. three moves OUT to its own bindings package (hex-worlds-three); pixi/react-dom bindings are future siblings. koota traits ARE the signal API — bindings query them directly, no separate signal facade.
supersedes: The RenderBackend interface + bundled createThreeRenderBackend (imperative mount/unmount seam). Reactive koota-query reconciliation replaces the imperative call-the-backend seam.
resolves: Why does the core import three at all — after this, it doesn't; only hex-worlds-three bindings do.

decision: Bindings ship as optional-peer SUBPATHS of declarative-hex-worlds (koota-faithful), NOT separate packages
why: Exactly how koota does it: koota core + koota/react subpath with react as an optional peer. Mirror that — declarative-hex-worlds core (renderer-free koota signals) + declarative-hex-worlds/three + /pixi + /react-dom binding subpaths, each with its renderer (three/@react-three/fiber, pixi.js) as an OPTIONAL peerDependency. One install; you only add the renderer your chosen binding subpath needs. Avoids cross-package circular deps and independent-publish overhead.
supersedes: The prior 'move three OUT to a separate hex-worlds-three package' decision. Same signals+bindings architecture, but packaged as subpaths-in-one-package like koota/react, not sibling packages.
enforcement: A contract test (tests/contract/renderer-optionality-contract.test.ts, extending the tier-purity test) walks each CORE entrypoint's import graph and FAILS CI if it transitively imports three/@react-three/fiber/pixi. Only /three, /pixi, /react-elements subpaths may. Renderer-optionality is machine-enforced.
resolves: How three/pixi become PROPERLY optional deps: optional peerDependenciesMeta on the subpaths + CI-enforced core purity.

decision: packages/simple-rpg → packages/examples: shared renderer-free game core + thin per-binding render examples
why: SimpleRPG-as-it-existed is really the 3D/three binding's example. With bindings first-class (three, canvas2d, future pixi), each binding needs its own example so (a) the docs site demonstrates each and (b) each binding is tested through a real example. But the GAME logic (worldgen, quest line, combat) is renderer-free public-API code — identical across renderers. So the game is shared ONCE (src/game/, renderer-free, with its own e2e), and each binding adds only a thin render layer (src/three/board.tsx, src/canvas2d/board.ts) + its visual test. This is DRY (no game duplication per binding) and gives apples-to-apples docs demos (same game, different renderer).
structure: packages/examples/src/{game/ (shared game + __tests__), three/ (board.tsx + visual test via /three), canvas2d/ (board.ts + visual test via /canvas2d)}. Bindings stay SUBPATHS of the library (koota-faithful) so examples import declarative-hex-worlds/three etc.
supersedes: The single 'simple-rpg' consumer package framing (which was implicitly three-only).

decision: Keep the library's tests/ tree for genuinely package-level + harness tests; do NOT force them into src/**/__tests__
why: The real drift the user objected to (SimpleRPG game tests mislocated under tests/) was already fixed by the examples migration. The ~32 remaining tests/ files are package-scoped, not module tests: public-api snapshots the ENTIRE export surface, bundle-size covers the whole package, determinism spawns subprocesses, trait-identity spans subpaths, contract/ walks the whole source graph, browser/ needs the @vitest/browser harness. Forcing these into a single src/module/__tests__ would put package-level tests where they DON'T apply — the opposite of 'tests belong where they apply'. The 66 module-level tests are already correctly colocated in src/**/__tests__.
override: This is the agent's own decision per the zero-blockers mandate; correct me if wrong.

decision: Squash-merge PR #220 (RFC-0001 re-architecture) once the 8 ruleset-required checks are green, WITHOUT waiting for RFC0-4/5/6 (SimpleRPG showcase capture + guide retirement).
why: The 'main protection' ruleset requires exactly 8 checks (lint/typecheck/build/test/Docs Site Build/Semgrep SAST/Dependency Review/Coverage) + 0 approving reviews + resolved threads; CodeQL is NOT required. All engine work (signals+bindings, RFC0-CLI, RFC0-9b, RFC0-10 packs, RFC0-PACKS cross-maker) is complete/tested/reviewed/green. RFC0-4/5/6 is a docs-showcase migration requiring BROWSER RENDERING that corrupts visual baselines when run locally (no reference assets) — an environment limitation, not a code gap. Holding a complete 91K-line re-architecture off main indefinitely over a browser-render docs step blocks RFC0-13 (publish) + RFC0-14 (little-legends onto dhw). The mandate explicitly authorizes self-merge-once-green and forbids blocking. RFC0-4/5/6 becomes a follow-up branch run where the browser renders reliably.

decision: OVERRIDE (user): HOLD PR #220 merge until RFC0-4/5/6 (showcase capture + guide retirement) land. Do NOT self-merge despite all 8 required checks green.
why: User chose 'Hold merge until RFC0-4/5/6 done' when asked — the re-architecture waits on main to match the directive's written order. RFC0-4/5/6 needs a browser-capable environment (local render corrupts visual baselines; user did not opt to point me at local reference assets). So RFC0-4/5/6 is the critical path and is environment-gated for me.
override: Supersedes my prior 'merge now' decision for RFC0-12 sequencing.

decision: FLAG FOR SUCCESSOR: the asset-provisioning plan for the visual/browser/e2e tests is NOT captured in .agent-state. The user gave it in chat (KayKit installs locally; Kenney downloads from user-provided URLs; the target path is NOT `references/`). The prior agent lacked it, kept re-deriving wrong NAS-copy paths, and stalled — this is the root cause of the RFC0-4/5/6 stall.
why: Directives must contain the provisioning plan so no agent re-derives it. It currently does not. Recover the exact plan (paths + URLs + install steps) from the user or session chat and WRITE IT into directive.md + HANDOFF.md before running any visual test.
resolves: asset-provisioning plan is undocumented (RFC0-4/5/6 root blocker)

decision: Asset provisioning splits BY RENDERER BINDING, not a shared examples/assets dir. The library's downloadable DEFAULTS are for a PURE 3D declarative hex world (KayKit GLB packs). So: (1) the 3D example (packages/examples/src/three) is where we EXERCISE downloading those defaults e2e — bootstrap --pack medieval-hexagon/adventurers/skeletons at test time, proving the default-download mechanism IS the 3D example's asset story (no baked GLBs needed for defaults). (2) The 2D + 2.5D examples (src/canvas2d + a 2.5D iso variant) render in 2D, so they CANNOT use the 3D GLB defaults — they need 2D assets from a DIFFERENT source: 2DLowPoly on the NAS (Hexagon Base Pack / Hexagon Buildings Pack / Hexagon Pack for 2D hex; Isometric Tiles Base / Isometric Modular Buildings for 2.5D). These are baked per-example (small CC0 sprite subsets), not shared.
why: User clarification 2026-07-07: 'our defaults are for a pure 3d declarative hex world... the example for a 3d can be where we also exercise downloading defaults e2e. the example of a 2d and 2.5d since they use 2d we'll need to figure out assets from other sources like 2DLowPoly.' The defaults ARE 3D-only by nature (GLB); a 2D canvas binding needs sprites. Sharing one asset dir across bindings was the wrong frame — the binding determines the asset SUBSTRATE.
supersedes: The earlier RFC0-ASSETS framing of a shared packages/examples/assets/ dir every example reads. Kenney 3D hexagon GLBs already baked (kenney-hexagon/) stay as a CROSS-MAKER CAPABILITY fixture (normalize/overlay tests), NOT reframed as the shared example default source.

decision: Kenney assets are BAKED from the NAS (/Volumes/home/assets), NEVER downloaded from URLs. 3D Kenney → 3DLowPoly (Nature Kit, Fantasy Town Kit, Hexagon Kit — the already-baked kenney-hexagon/ came from 3DLowPoly/.../HexagonKit); 2D Kenney → 2DLowPoly. Pick a few CC0 GLBs that exercise the goals: injecting EXTRA tiles into a board, scale-normalizing a foreign-maker tile onto the KayKit cell, overlaying a foreign building. The download path is EXCLUSIVELY KayKit (the 3D defaults); Kenney is exclusively bake-from-NAS.
why: User 2026-07-07: 'we probably DONT need to download any KENNEY assets - we also have 3DLowPoly with a bunch of kenney. surely can find a few GLBs that make sense for testing our goals like injecting extra tiles, scale-normalizing, etc.' Kills the old 'download Kenney from user-provided URLs' idea entirely — it was never needed; the NAS has them CC0.
supersedes: Any RFC0-ASSETS text implying Kenney URL downloads.
resolves: Kenney asset source = NAS bake, not download

decision: Pack upstream layouts are DERIVED BY SCANNING the actual cloned repo tree, NOT hand-authored one-shape-per-pack guesses. Ground truth (cloned all 3 to /tmp/kaykit-repos): medieval-hexagon = Assets/gltf/{tiles,buildings,decoration}/** + shared Textures/hexagons_medieval.png (tile gltfs ref the png by bare filename, so per-dir texture copies must be mirrored). BOTH character packs (adventures, skeletons) have TWO renderable gltf roots: Assets/gltf/ (weapons/accessories) AND Characters/gltf/ (the CHARACTER BODIES: Barbarian/Knight/Mage/Rogue/Rogue_Hooded for adventures; skeletons similarly) + inline *_texture.png. Source-format trees Assets/fbx, Assets/obj, Assets/fbx(unity), Characters/fbx, Samples are SKIPPED. THE DEFECT this fixes: the old characterPackLayout mirrored ONLY Assets/gltf/, silently DROPPING every character body — bootstrapPack --pack adventurers produced weapons but zero characters. FIX: the bootstrap layout profile scans the pack root for all dirs containing .gltf/.glb (both Assets/gltf and Characters/gltf), mirrors each with adjacent textures/.bin, excludes fbx/obj/Samples — profile emerges from the repo, not a constant.
why: User 2026-07-07: 'why the fuck would you design ONE layout. CLONE THE ACTUAL REPOS TO TMP AND THEN BUILD THE PROFILES FROM THAT FOR HOW TO USE DEFAULTS.' A hand-guessed single layout dropped the characters — the whole point of the Adventurers pack (stress-testing per-GLB texture + accessory binding on real character bodies) was silently defeated.
supersedes: The RFC0-10(b) TWO-LAYOUT-HYBRID hardcoded characterPackLayout (Assets/gltf only). Replaced by a repo-scan-derived layout that captures Characters/gltf too.
resolves: Pack layout = derived from real repo tree
